task_completion.description: Verify that at least 4 partition walls have been drawn to create an enclosed walk-in cooler and a back-of-house wet workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D floor plan or 3D view for new interior walls that divide the open-plan unit into distinct enclosed areas (a cooler and a workspace).

task_completion.description: Verify that at least 2 doors have been placed for access to the enclosed rooms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for door icons/swing arcs in the 2D floor plan or 3D door models placed within the newly created partition walls.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least 3 rooms are defined and named appropriately for a floral shop, and at least 2 have distinct floor colors/textures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for text labels on the 2D plan indicating room names like 'Retail Storefront', 'Walk-in Cooler', or 'Workspace'. Check the 3D view or plan for distinct floor colors or textures applied to at least two of these areas.

task_completion.description: Verify the workspace is furnished with at least 2 sinks and 3 large desks or tables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the designated workspace area for 3D models or 2D icons of at least 2 sinks and 3 large tables/desks.

task_completion.description: Verify the retail area is furnished with at least 5 display shelves, a checkout counter, and 3 chairs or sofas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the retail area for at least 5 shelving units, a counter/desk acting as a checkout, and at least 3 seating items (chairs/sofas).

task_completion.description: Verify the shop contains at least 10 botanical items and appears to have a minimum of 35 total furniture items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a dense placement of plants, flowers, or botanical items (at least 10) scattered throughout the shop, and a generally well-furnished space indicating around 35 total items.

task_completion.description: Verify the final design has been saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the absence of an asterisk (*) next to the filename 'floral_shop_starter.sh3d' in the window title bar, or a recently closed save dialog.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical interface to design the floor plan.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application open with the 2D plan and 3D view actively being edited, rather than terminal commands modifying XML files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used items from the built-in catalog.
integrity.visual_evidence: Furniture and botanical items should look like standard Sweet Home 3D 3D models dragged from the left-hand catalog pane, not pasted 2D images or text placeholders.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Sweet Home 3D software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window, menus, catalog, and toolbars of Sweet Home 3D must be visible in the screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses a starter file named 'floral_shop_starter.sh3d'. This starter file already contains 4 pre-existing rooms with French names ('terrasse', 'salon', 'cuisine', 'ch. 1') and 2 distinct floor colors. To properly verify the room creation requirement, ensure the agent has defined new rooms relevant to a floral shop (e.g., 'Retail Storefront', 'Walk-in Cooler', 'Workspace') rather than just relying on the pre-existing ones. Additionally, visually confirm the placement of at least 2 doors, as the automated script verifier has a known bug that fails to count doors.
